description: Replace the bouncing dots Loader in ToolRow with HDD-style activity flash indicators on PluginIcon and ToolIcon green dots, with soft fade-out when activity stops

userStories.title: Add CSS keyframes for HDD-style activity flash animation
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a reusable CSS animation class that produces a hard-drive activity light effect (rapid on/off flashing that speeds up with frequency and soft-fades out when removed) so that icon status dots can indicate tool activity
acceptanceCriteria: A new @keyframes `activity-flash` is defined in `platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/styles.css` that rapidly pulses opacity between 1.0 and 0.3 with a fast cycle (around 300-400ms per full cycle, ease-in-out)
A new utility class `.animate-activity-flash` applies the animation with infinite iteration
A new @keyframes `activity-fade-out` is defined that transitions opacity from 1.0 to 0.0 over ~500ms with ease-out timing, for the soft-end behavior when activity stops
A new utility class `.animate-activity-fade-out` applies the fade-out animation once (fill-mode forwards so it stays at opacity 0 after completing)
The existing `pulse-dot` and `tool-pulse` keyframes and their classes are removed since they are unused
npm run build passes
npm run type-check passes
npm run lint passes
npm run knip passes
npm run test passes

userStories.title: Add activity flash to PluginIcon status dot and BrowserToolsCard icon
userStories.description: As a user, I want the green ready dot on plugin icons and the browser tools icon to flash like an HDD activity light when any tool in that plugin is being invoked, with a soft fade-out when the invocation ends, so I can see at a glance which plugin is working
acceptanceCriteria: PluginIcon accepts a new optional `active` boolean prop (default false)
When `active` is true AND `tabState` is 'ready' (and no update), the green status dot gets the `animate-activity-flash` CSS class, causing it to flash rapidly
When `active` transitions from true to false, the dot gets the `animate-activity-fade-out` class for ~500ms, then returns to the normal static green dot — this is the soft-end behavior
The `active` prop has no visual effect when `tabState` is not 'ready' or when `hasUpdate` is true (those states have their own indicators)
In PluginCard.tsx, compute `hasActiveTool` as `pluginTools.some(t => activeTools.has(`${plugin.name}:${t.name}`))` and pass it as the `active` prop to PluginIcon
In BrowserToolsCard.tsx, compute `hasActiveTool` as `tools.some(t => activeTools.has(`browser:${t.name}`))` and pass it as the `active` prop to PluginIcon
The activity flash also works in SearchResults (which renders PluginCard and BrowserToolsCard with activeTools — no changes needed there since props flow through)
npm run build passes
npm run type-check passes
npm run lint passes
npm run knip passes
npm run test passes

userStories.title: Add activity flash dot to ToolIcon for individual tool invocations
userStories.description: As a user, I want each tool's icon to show a small green activity dot at the bottom-right corner (like the PluginIcon ready dot) that flashes when that specific tool is being invoked, with the same soft fade-out, so I can see which exact tool is running
acceptanceCriteria: ToolIcon accepts a new optional `active` boolean prop (default false)

When `active` is true, a small green dot appears at the bottom-right corner of the ToolIcon (positioned absolutely, same style as PluginIcon's status dot: `bg-success border-card rounded-full border-2`, sized proportionally to the 24px icon — about 8px diameter)
The dot flashes with the `animate-activity-flash` class while active
When `active` transitions from true to false, the dot soft-fades out using `animate-activity-fade-out` over ~500ms, then is removed from the DOM
The dot does not appear at all when the tool has never been active (no invisible placeholder — zero layout impact since it's absolutely positioned)
The ToolIcon container needs `relative` added to its className to support the absolutely-positioned dot
npm run build passes
npm run type-check passes
npm run lint passes
npm run knip passes
npm run test passes
userStories.notes:
Edit `platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/ToolIcon.tsx`.

The ToolIcon is 24x24 (`h-6 w-6`). The activity dot should be ~8px diameter, positioned at `bottom: -2, right: -2` — same pattern as PluginIcon's StatusIndicator for the 32px icon.

1. Add `active?: boolean` to `ToolIconProps` (default `false`).
2. Add `relative` to the container div's className (currently `flex h-6 w-6 shrink-0 items-center justify-center rounded border-2 transition-colors`).
3. Use the same `useRef`/`useEffect`/`useState` fade-out pattern from US-002.
4. Conditionally render the dot when `active || fadingOut`:

```tsx
{(active || fadingOut) && (
  <div
    className={cn(
      'bg-success border-card absolute rounded-full border-2',
      active && 'animate-activity-flash',
      fadingOut && !active && 'animate-activity-fade-out',
    )}
    style={{ width: 8, height: 8, bottom: -2, right: -2 }}
  />
)}
```

The dot is absolutely positioned outside the icon box, so it has zero layout impact — no shift when it appears or disappears. Import `cn` from `../lib/cn.js`, `useState`, `useEffect`, `useRef` from React.

Note: Since ToolIcon now uses hooks, it must become a function body (not a const arrow returning JSX). Convert from `const ToolIcon = (...) => (...)` to `const ToolIcon = (...) => { ... return (...); }`.

userStories.title: Remove Loader from ToolRow and wire up active prop to ToolIcon
userStories.description: As a user, I want the old bouncing dots animation removed from tool rows and replaced with the new ToolIcon activity dot, so the activity indication is consistent across the UI
acceptanceCriteria: The `Loader` import is removed from ToolRow.tsx
The `{active && <Loader .../>}` render is removed from ToolRow.tsx
The `bg-accent/20` background highlight on active tool rows is removed (the activity dot on the icon is now the sole indicator)
The `active` prop is passed through to `<ToolIcon active={active} .../>` instead
The ToolRow still accepts the `active` boolean prop (it passes it to ToolIcon)
npm run build passes
npm run type-check passes
npm run lint passes
npm run knip passes
npm run test passes
userStories.notes:
Edit `platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/ToolRow.tsx`.

Current code (lines 1-49):
- Line 1: `import { Loader } from './retro/Loader.js';` — **remove this import**
- Lines 25-29: The `cn()` call includes `active && 'bg-accent/20'` — **remove the active conditional** so the className is just:
  ```ts
  cn(
    'border-border flex items-center gap-2 border-b px-3 py-2 transition-colors last:border-b-0',
    enabled ? 'hover:bg-primary/10' : 'hover:bg-muted/50',
  )
  ```
- Line 30: `<ToolIcon icon={icon} enabled={enabled} />` — **add `active={active}`**: `<ToolIcon icon={icon} enabled={enabled} active={active} />`
- Lines 39-47: The right-side `<div className="flex shrink-0 items-center gap-2">` currently contains `{active && <Loader .../>}` and `<Switch>`. **Remove the Loader** render. The `gap-2` in the wrapper can stay (it only affects spacing when there are multiple children, which is now just the Switch — harmless).

After this change, ToolRow's `active` prop flows to ToolIcon, which shows the green flashing dot. The Loader is no longer used in ToolRow.

userStories.title: Update E2E test for tool invocation animation to check activity dot instead of Loader
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the E2E test that verifies tool invocation animation to check for the new activity dot indicator instead of the old Loader component, so the test suite stays green
acceptanceCriteria: The E2E test in `e2e/side-panel-data-flow.e2e.ts` (the 'shows spinner during tool call and removes it after' test) is updated to check for the activity dot instead of the Loader
The test verifies that a green dot with the `animate-activity-flash` class appears on the ToolIcon during a tool invocation
The test verifies the dot disappears (or fades out) after the tool call completes
The `activeRowLocator` check for `.bg-accent/20` is removed (that class is no longer applied)
npm run build passes
npm run type-check passes
npm run lint passes
npm run knip passes
npm run test passes
npm run test:e2e passes
userStories.notes:
Edit `e2e/side-panel-data-flow.e2e.ts`, specifically the test starting at line 355.

Current test locators (lines 422-423):
```ts
const loaderLocator = sidePanelPage.locator('[role="status"][aria-label="Loading..."]');
const activeRowLocator = sidePanelPage.locator('.bg-accent\\/20');
```

Replace with a locator for the activity dot. The activity dot is a `div` with class `animate-activity-flash` and `bg-success` inside a ToolIcon. A good locator:
```ts
const activityDotLocator = sidePanelPage.locator('.animate-activity-flash');
```

Update the assertions:
- Line 426: `await expect(loaderLocator).toBeHidden(...)` → `await expect(activityDotLocator).toBeHidden(...)` (or `.toHaveCount(0)` since the dot is not in DOM when inactive)
- Line 432: `await expect(loaderLocator).toBeVisible(...)` → `await expect(activityDotLocator).toBeVisible(...)`
- Line 433: Remove the `activeRowLocator` assertion (no more `bg-accent/20` highlight)
- Line 440: `await expect(loaderLocator).toBeHidden(...)` → `await expect(activityDotLocator).toBeHidden(...)` (after fade-out completes, the dot should not be visible — allow extra timeout for the 500ms fade-out)
- Line 441: Remove the `activeRowLocator` assertion

Also update the test title and the file's opening comment (line 6) to reference 'activity dot' instead of 'spinner'.

Note: The activity dot fades out over 500ms after invocationEnd, so the 'hidden' check after tool completion may need a slightly longer timeout (e.g., 12_000 instead of 10_000) to account for the fade-out animation completing.

userStories.title: Update Storybook stories for ToolRow, ToolIcon, and PluginIcon to showcase activity state
userStories.description: As a developer, I want Storybook stories updated to showcase the new activity dot behavior on ToolIcon and PluginIcon, and the updated ToolRow without the Loader, so the component library is accurate
acceptanceCriteria: PluginIcon.stories.tsx has a new 'Active' story showing the icon with `active={true}` and `tabState='ready'`
ToolIcon.stories.tsx has a new 'Active' story showing the icon with `active={true}`
ToolRow.stories.tsx 'Active' story still works (it passes `active={true}` which now shows via the ToolIcon dot instead of Loader)
The ToolList story in ToolRow.stories.tsx still shows one active tool (visually confirmed by the green dot on its icon)
npm run build passes
npm run type-check passes
npm run lint passes
npm run knip passes
npm run test passes
userStories.notes:
Three story files to update:

### PluginIcon.stories.tsx
Add after the existing exports:
```ts
const Active: Story = {
  args: { pluginName: 'slack', displayName: 'Slack', tabState: 'ready', size: 32, active: true },
};
```
Add `Active` to the exports.

### ToolIcon.stories.tsx
Add after the existing exports:
```ts
const Active: Story = { args: { icon: 'send', active: true } };
const ActiveDisabled: Story = { args: { icon: 'send', enabled: false, active: true } };
```
Add to exports. Also update the Gallery story to include one active icon in the row.

### ToolRow.stories.tsx
The existing `Active` story at line 25 (`args: { ...Enabled.args, active: true }`) should still work since `active` still flows through ToolRow to ToolIcon. No changes needed beyond verifying it compiles.

The `ToolList` story at line 46 already has `active={t.name === 'search'}` on one row — this should now show the green dot on the Search tool's icon instead of the old Loader dots.

All existing stories should continue to work without modification since the `active` prop still exists on ToolRow.
